canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, SAT is characterized as a standardized test, originally for U.S. college admissions, that has been repurposed to evaluate language model performance. Its primary documented use is as a benchmark to measure the construct of 'Mathematical reasoning'. Papers group SAT with other mathematics-related benchmarks like GSM8k and MATH to assess this capability across diverse domains and difficulty levels. The test is also used for comparing model performance against human levels, with one paper noting that a model's scores on the SAT are "comparable to those of human test-takers" ('A Mathematical Framework for AI-Human Integration in Work').
papers: A Mathematical Framework for AI-Human Integration in Work
Overcoming Vocabulary Mismatch: Vocabulary-agnostic Teacher Guided Language Modeling
PENCIL: Long Thoughts with Short Memory

definitions.text: A standardized test widely used for college admissions in the United States, used here to evaluate model performance.
definitions.paper: A Mathematical Framework for AI-Human Integration in Work

relationships.paper: Harnessing Diversity for Important Data Selection in Pretraining Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: We compare the model performances on nine mathematics-related benchmarks used in RHO-1 (Lin et al., 2024) and ProX (Zhou et al., 2024a). The evaluation is conducted using the same implementation and includes few-shot chain-of-thought (CoT) examples.

iclr25: Harnessing Diversity for Important Data Selection in Pretraining Large Language Models
icml25: Overcoming Vocabulary Mismatch: Vocabulary-agnostic Teacher Guided Language Modeling
